title: Verify Brand Claims Response (Bulk)
description: Bulk response for `verify_brand_claims`. `results[]` is positionally aligned with the request's `claims[]` (zip-by-index). Per-result success carries `claim_type` + `status` + per-claim-type `details`; per-result failure carries an `error` field on that single result without failing the batch. Top-level `errors[]` is reserved for batch-level failures (auth, rate-limit, malformed request) — in that case `results` is absent. Top-level `Cache-Control` represents the lowest-common max-age across the batch; individual results MAY carry their own staleness signals out-of-band.

results.description: Per-claim results, positionally aligned with the request's `claims[]`. Each entry is either a per-claim success (claim_type + status + optional details/context_note) or a per-claim error (error field only).

oneOf.title: VerifyBrandClaimsError
oneOf.description: Batch-level failure — the entire request was rejected. Use per-result `error` on the success arm for per-claim failures that should not fail the batch.

result_entry.description: One entry in `results[]`. Either a per-claim success (claim_type + status + optional details/context_note) or a per-claim error (error field only). Mirrors the single-target `verify_brand_claim` response success arm shape.

claim_type.description: Echoes the request item's claim_type for caller-side routing.

status.description: Verification status for this claim. Not every status applies to every claim_type — see the single-target task page for the applicable subset.

details.description: Per-claim-type response fields. Shape varies — see the single-target task page for each claim_type's expected fields.

context_note.description: Public — free-text context the brand chooses to surface.

oneOf.title: VerifyBrandClaimsResultError
oneOf.description: Per-claim failure carried inline so a batch can return partial results. Distinct from the batch-level errors arm.
